GitHub v3 REST API OpenAPI document (version 1.1.4).

- `openapi`: 3.0.3
- `info`:
  - `version`: 1.1.4
  - `title`: GitHub v3 REST API
  - `description`: GitHub's v3 REST API.
  - `license`: MIT (`https://spdx.org/licenses/MIT`)
  - `termsOfService`: `https://docs.github.com/articles/github-terms-of-service`
  - `contact`: Support (`https://support.github.com/contact?tags=dotcom-rest-api`)
  - `x-github-plan`: `api.github.com`
- `servers`:
  - `https://api.github.com`
- `externalDocs`:
  - description: GitHub v3 REST API
  - url: `https://docs.github.com/rest/`
- `paths`: {} (no standard REST paths defined in this excerpt)

Webhook definitions under `x-webhooks`:

`branch-protection-configuration-disabled` webhook  
- HTTP method: `post`
- `summary`: Triggered when there is a change to branch protection configurations for a repository, specifically when all branch protections are disabled.
- Docs references:
  - Event docs: `https://docs.github.com/webhooks/webhook-events-and-payloads#branch_protection_configuration`
  - General protected branches info: about protected branches docs.
  - API management:
    - GraphQL: BranchProtectionRule object.
    - REST: Branch protection endpoints.
- Permission requirement to subscribe:
  - GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the `Administration` repository permission.
- `description`: All branch protections were disabled for a repository.
- `operationId`: `branch-protection-configuration/disabled`

Request metadata (headers):

- `User-Agent` (header):
  - Example: `GitHub-Hookshot/123abc`
  - Type: string
- `X-Github-Hook-Id` (header):
  - Example: `12312312`
  - Type: string
- `X-Github-Event` (header):
  - Example: `branch_protection_configuration`
  - Type: string
- `X-Github-Hook-Installation-Target-Id` (header):
  - Example: `123123`
  - Type: string
- `X-Github-Hook-Installation-Target-Type` (header):
  - Example: `repository`
  - Type: string
- `X-GitHub-Delivery` (header):
  - Example: `0b989ba4-242f-11e5-81e1-c7b6966d2516`
  - Type: string
- `X-Hub-Signature-256` (header):
  - Example: `sha256=6dcb09b5b57875f334f61aebed695e2e4193db5e`
  - Type: string

Request body:

- Required: `true`
- Content type: `application/json`
- Schema:
  - `title`: `branch protection configuration disabled event`
  - `type`: `object`
  - `properties`:

    `action`  
    - Type: string  
    - Enum: `disabled`  

    `enterprise` (Enterprise; optional, present when webhook is configured on an enterprise or an org that is part of an enterprise)  
    - Description: Represents a GitHub enterprise account.  
    - Type: object  
    - Properties:
      - `description`: short description; string, nullable.
      - `html_url`: string, uri; example: `https://github.com/enterprises/octo-business`
      - `website_url`: enterprise website URL; string, uri, nullable.
      - `id`: integer; unique identifier; example: `42`
      - `node_id`: string; example: `MDEwOlJlcG9zaXRvcnkxMjk2MjY5`
      - `name`: string; enterprise name; example: `Octo Business`
      - `slug`: string; URL slug; example: `octo-business`
      - `created_at`: string, date-time, nullable; example: `2019-01-26T19:01:12Z`
      - `updated_at`: string, date-time, nullable; example: `2019-01-26T19:14:43Z`
      - `avatar_url`: string, uri.
    - Required: `id`, `node_id`, `name`, `slug`, `html_url`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `avatar_url`.

    `installation` (Simple Installation; present when sent to a GitHub App)  
    - Description: GitHub App installation information, included when the event is for a GitHub App.  
    - Type: object  
    - Properties:
      - `id`: integer; installation ID; example: `1`
      - `node_id`: string; global node ID; example: `MDQ6VXNlcjU4MzIzMQ==`
    - Required: `id`, `node_id`.

    `organization` (Organization Simple)  
    - Description: A GitHub organization. Present when the webhook is configured for an organization or the repo is org-owned.  
    - Type: object  
    - Properties:
      - `login`: string; example: `github`
      - `id`: integer; example: `1`
      - `node_id`: string; example: `MDEyOk9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbjE=`
      - `url`: string, uri; `https://api.github.com/orgs/github`
      - `repos_url`: string, uri
      - `events_url`: string, uri
      - `hooks_url`: string
      - `issues_url`: string
      - `members_url`: string
      - `public_members_url`: string
      - `avatar_url`: string; example: `https://github.com/images/error/octocat_happy.gif`
      - `description`: string, nullable; example: `A great organization`
    - Required: `login`, `url`, `id`, `node_id`, `repos_url`, `events_url`, `hooks_url`, `issues_url`, `members_url`, `public_members_url`, `avatar_url`, `description`.

    `repository` (Repository)  
    - Description: The GitHub repository where the event occurred.  
    - Type: object  
    - Key properties (many URL fields follow GitHub standard REST patterns):
      - `id`: integer (int64); unique repo ID; example: `42`
      - `node_id`: string; example: `MDEwOlJlcG9zaXRvcnkxMjk2MjY5`
      - `name`: string; repo name; example: `Team Environment`
      - `full_name`: string; `octocat/Hello-World`
      - `license` (License Simple; nullable):
        - `key`: string; example: `mit`
        - `name`: string; example: `MIT License`
        - `url`: string, uri, nullable; example: `https://api.github.com/licenses/mit`
        - `spdx_id`: string, nullable; example: `MIT`
        - `node_id`: string; example: `MDc6TGljZW5zZW1pdA==`
        - `html_url`: string, uri
        - Required: `key`, `name`, `url`, `spdx_id`, `node_id`.
      - `organization` (Simple User; nullable): GitHub user/organization that owns the repo (standard user fields as below).
      - `forks`: integer
      - `permissions`:
        - `admin`: boolean
        - `pull`: boolean
        - `triage`: boolean
        - `push`: boolean
        - `maintain`: boolean
        - Required: `admin`, `pull`, `push`
      - `owner` (Simple User):  
        - Description: A GitHub user.  
        - Fields: `name` (nullable), `email` (nullable), `login`, `id` (int64), `node_id`, `avatar_url` (uri), `gravatar_id` (nullable), `url` (uri), `html_url` (uri), `followers_url` (uri), `following_url`, `gists_url`, `starred_url`, `subscriptions_url` (uri), `organizations_url` (uri), `repos_url` (uri), `events_url`, `received_events_url` (uri), `type` (example: `User`), `site_admin` (boolean), `starred_at` (string, example timestamp), `user_view_type` (string, example: `public`).  
        - Required: `avatar_url`, `events_url`, `followers_url`, `following_url`, `gists_url`, `gravatar_id`, `html_url`, `id`, `node_id`, `login`, `organizations_url`, `received_events_url`, `repos_url`, `site_admin`, `starred_url`, `subscriptions_url`, `type`, `url`.
      - `private`: boolean; whether repo is private; default `false`.
      - `html_url`: string, uri; example: `https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World`
      - `description`: string, nullable; example: `This your first repo!`
      - `fork`: boolean
      - `url`: string, uri; example: `https://api.github.com/repos/octocat/Hello-World`

      - Multiple standard GitHub API URL templates (strings, some with URI format) for repo resources, including:
        - `archive_url`, `assignees_url`, `blobs_url`, `branches_url`, `collaborators_url`, `comments_url`, `commits_url`, `compare_url`, `contents_url`, `contributors_url`, `deployments_url`, `downloads_url`, `events_url`, `forks_url`, `git_commits_url`, `git_refs_url`, `git_tags_url`, `git_url`, `issue_comment_url`, `issue_events_url`, `issues_url`, `keys_url`, `labels_url`, `languages_url`, `merges_url`, `milestones_url`, `notifications_url`, `pulls_url`, `releases_url`, `ssh_url`, `stargazers_url`, `statuses_url`, `subscribers_url`, `subscription_url`, `tags_url`, `teams_url`, `trees_url`, `clone_url`, `mirror_url` (uri, nullable), `hooks_url` (uri), `svn_url` (uri), `homepage` (uri).

nodes.output:
openapi: 3.0.3; describes GitHub v3 REST API plus webhook schemas.

Core API metadata:
- `info`: version `1.1.4`, title `GitHub v3 REST API`, description "GitHub's v3 REST API."
- `license`: MIT with SPDX URL.
- `termsOfService`: https://docs.github.com/articles/github-terms-of-service
- `contact`: name `Support`, URL for contacting GitHub Support with `dotcom-rest-api` tag.
- `x-github-plan`: `api.github.com`
- `servers`: single server `https://api.github.com`
- `externalDocs`: links to REST API docs at https://docs.github.com/rest/
- `paths`: empty (no REST endpoints described in this snippet).

Webhook section (`x-webhooks`):

Event: `branch-protection-configuration-disabled`
- Method: `post`
- Summary: Triggered when branch protection configurations change for a repository, specifically when all branch protections are disabled.
- Description:
  - All branch protections were disabled for a repository.
  - References docs:
    - About protected branches.
    - GraphQL `BranchProtectionRule` object docs.
    - REST branch protection API docs.
  - Subscription requirements: GitHub App must have at least read-level `Administration` permission on the repository.
- `operationId`: `branch-protection-configuration/disabled`
- `externalDocs`: link to webhook docs section `#branch_protection_configuration`.

Request:
- `requestBody`: `required: true`, content type `application/json`.
- JSON schema: title `branch protection configuration disabled event`, `type: object`, with top-level properties:

  1) `action`
  - Type: `string`
  - Enum: `["disabled"]` (indicates branch protection configuration was disabled).

  2) `enterprise` (ref_1)
  - Title: `Enterprise`; describes an enterprise account on GitHub.
  - Present in payload when webhook is configured on an enterprise account or on an organization that is part of an enterprise.
  - More info: "About enterprise accounts" docs.
  - Type: `object`
  - Properties:
    - `description`: short description; `string`, nullable.
    - `html_url`: enterprise HTML URL; `string`, `uri`, example `https://github.com/enterprises/octo-business`.
    - `website_url`: enterprise website; `string`, `uri`, nullable.
    - `id`: unique integer identifier, example `42`.
    - `node_id`: global node ID, `string`, example `MDEwOlJlcG9zaXRvcnkxMjk2MjY5`.
    - `name`: name of enterprise; `string`, example `Octo Business`.
    - `slug`: URL slug identifier; `string`, example `octo-business`.
    - `created_at`: `string`, `date-time`, nullable, example `2019-01-26T19:01:12Z`.
    - `updated_at`: `string`, `date-time`, nullable, example `2019-01-26T19:14:43Z`.
    - `avatar_url`: enterprise avatar; `string`, `uri`.
  - Required: `id`, `node_id`, `name`, `slug`, `html_url`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `avatar_url`.

  3) `installation` (ref_2)
  - Title: `Simple Installation`; describes the GitHub App installation.
  - Present when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App.
  - More info: "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps" docs.
  - Type: `object`
  - Properties:
    - `id`: integer installation ID, example `1`.
    - `node_id`: global node ID of installation, `string`, example `MDQ6VXNlcjU4MzIzMQ==`.
  - Required: `id`, `node_id`.

  4) `organization` (ref_3)
  - Title: `Organization Simple`; describes an organization object.
  - Present when webhook is configured for an organization or when the activity occurs in an organization-owned repository.
  - Type: `object`
  - Properties:
    - `login`: organization login; `string`, example `github`.
    - `id`: integer ID, example `1`.
    - `node_id`: global node ID, `string`, example `MDEyOk9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbjE=`.
    - `url`: API URL; `string`, `uri`, example `https://api.github.com/orgs/github`.
    - `repos_url`: `https://api.github.com/orgs/github/repos`.
    - `events_url`: `https://api.github.com/orgs/github/events`.
    - `hooks_url`: `https://api.github.com/orgs/github/hooks`.
    - `issues_url`: `https://api.github.com/orgs/github/issues`.
    - `members_url`: templated members URL, e.g. `.../members{/member}`.
    - `public_members_url`: templated public members URL.
    - `avatar_url`: avatar URL, example GitHub image.
    - `description`: organization description; `string`, nullable, example `A great organization`.
  - Required: `login`, `url`, `id`, `node_id`, `repos_url`, `events_url`, `hooks_url`, `issues_url`, `members_url`, `public_members_url`, `avatar_url`, `description`.

  5) `repository` (ref_4)
  - Title: `Repository`; describes the repository where the event occurred.
  - Type: `object`
  - Key properties:
    - `id`: 64-bit integer unique repository ID, example `42`.
    - `node_id`: global node ID, `string`.
    - `name`: repository name, e.g. `Team Environment`.
    - `full_name`: `owner/repo`, e.g. `octocat/Hello-World`.
    - `license` (ref_23, nullable):
      - Title: `License Simple`.
      - Properties: `key` (e.g. `mit`), `name` (e.g. `MIT License`), `url` (nullable license API URL), `spdx_id` (nullable, e.g. `MIT`), `node_id`, `html_url` (license HTML URL).
      - Required: `key`, `name`, `url`, `spdx_id`, `node_id`.
    - `organization` (ref_19, nullable): same `Simple User` schema as `owner`, representing owning organization/user:
      - Properties include: `name` (nullable), `email` (nullable), `login` (`octocat`), `id` (int64), `node_id`, `avatar_url`, `gravatar_id` (nullable), `url`, `html_url`, `followers_url`, `following_url`, `gists_url`, `starred_url`, `subscriptions_url`, `organizations_url`, `repos_url`, `events_url`, `received_events_url`, `type` (`User`), `site_admin` (boolean), `starred_at` (string timestamp), `user_view_type` (e.g. `public`).
      - Required: avatar/events/followers/following/gists/gravatar/html/id/node_id/login/organizations/received_events/repos/site_admin/starred/subscriptions/type/url.
    - `forks`: integer forks count.
    - `permissions`: object with booleans `admin`, `pull`, `triage`, `push`, `maintain`; required: `admin`, `pull`, `push`.
    - `owner` (ref_0): `Simple User` schema (same as `organization` above) for repository owner.
    - `private`: boolean, default `false`, indicates private vs public.
    - `html_url`: repository HTML URL, e.g. `https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World`.
    - `description`: repo description, nullable, example `This your first repo!`.
    - `fork`: boolean indicating whether repo is a fork.
    - `url`: API URL, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/octocat/Hello-World`.

  - URL/endpoint fields on `repository` (all `string`, many with `uri` format and sample templates):
    - `archive_url`: `.../{archive_format}{/ref}`
    - `assignees_url`: `.../assignees{/user}`
    - `blobs_url`: `.../git/blobs{/sha}`
    - `branches_url`: `.../branches{/branch}`
    - `collaborators_url`: `.../collaborators{/collaborator}`
    - `comments_url`: `.../comments{/number}`
    - `commits_url`: `.../commits{/sha}`
    - `compare_url`: `.../compare/{base}...{head}`
    - `contents_url`: `.../contents/{+path}`
    - `contributors_url`: contributors API URL.
    - `deployments_url`: deployments API URL.
    - `downloads_url`: downloads API URL.
    - `events_url`: repo events API URL.
    - `forks_url`: forks API URL.
    - `git_commits_url`: `.../git/commits{/sha}`
    - `git_refs_url`: `.../git/refs{/sha}`
    - `git_tags_url`: `.../git/tags{/sha}`
    - `git_url`: git protocol URL, e.g. `git:github.com/octocat/Hello-World.git`.
    - `issue_comment_url`: `.../issues/comments{/number}`
    - `issue_events_url`: `.../issues/events{/number}`
    - `issues_url`: `.../issues{/number}`
    - `keys_url`: `.../keys{/key_id}`
    - `labels_url`: `.../labels{/name}`
    - `languages_url`: languages API URL.
    - `merges_url`: merges API URL.
    - `milestones_url`: `.../milestones{/number}`
    - `notifications_url`: notifications with query parameters `{?since,all,participating}`.
    - `pulls_url`: `.../pulls{/number}`
    - `releases_url`: `.../releases{/id}`
    - `ssh_url`: SSH URL, e.g. `git@github.com:octocat/Hello-World.git`.
    - `stargazers_url`: stargazers API URL.
    - `statuses_url`: `.../statuses/{sha}`
    - `subscribers_url`: subscribers API URL.
    - `subscription_url`: subscription API URL.
    - `tags_url`: tags API URL.
    - `teams_url`: teams API URL.
    - `trees_url`: `.../git/trees{/sha}`
    - `clone_url`: HTTPS clone URL, e.g. `https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World.git`.
    - `mirror_url`: mirror URL; `uri`, nullable, e.g. `git:git.example.com/octocat/Hello-World`.
    - `hooks_url`: repository hooks API URL.
    - `svn_url`: Subversion URL, e.g. `https://svn.github.com/octocat/Hello-World`.
    - `homepage`: repository homepage; `string`, `uri`.

Headers for the webhook delivery (`parameters` on the `post` operation):
- `User-Agent`: header, example `GitHub-Hookshot/123abc`, type `string`.
- `X-Github-Hook-Id`: header, example `12312312`.
- `X-Github-Event`: header, indicates event type, example `branch_protection_configuration`.
- `X-Github-Hook-Installation-Target-Id`: header, example `123123`.
- `X-Github-Hook-Installation-Target-Type`: header, example `repository`.
- `X-GitHub-Delivery`: header, unique delivery ID, example GUID-like string.
- `X-Hub-Signature-256`: header, HMAC signature, example `sha256=6dcb09b5b57875f334f61aebed695e2e4193db5e`.

nodes.name: llm
llm
nodes.output:
OpenAI API v2.3.0 is a REST API with 241 endpoints at `https://api.openai.com` covering models, assistants, conversations, media, evals, projects, permissions, and realtime features. Full endpoint docs: `https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference` and per-endpoint summaries at `https://oapis.org/summary/openai/[idOrRoute]`.

Assistants  
Create and manage AI assistants that use models and tools and converse in threads.  
- Assistants: list, create (model + instructions), get, modify, delete.  
- Threads: create, get, modify, delete.  
- Messages: list in a thread, create, get, modify, delete.  
- Runs: list for a thread, create, get, modify, cancel (if in progress).  
- Run steps: list and get individual steps.  
- Tool outputs: submit tool outputs when a run is in `requires_action`/`submit_tool_outputs` state; all outputs must be submitted in one request.

Audio  
Convert between text and audio and manage voice-related assets.  
- Text to speech: generate audio from text, returning audio content or a streamed sequence.  
- Transcription: transcribe audio into source language, returning JSON/diarized JSON/verbose JSON or a stream.  
- Translation: translate audio to English.  
- Voice consents: list, upload, get, update metadata, delete consent recordings.  
- Custom voices: create a custom voice.

Audit Logs  
- List audit logs: list user actions and configuration changes within an organization.

Batch  
Run large batches of API requests asynchronously.  
- Batches: list, create (from uploaded file) and execute, retrieve, cancel in‑progress batches (status goes through `cancelling` to `cancelled`, partial output may be available).

Certificates  
Manage TLS-like certificates at organization and project levels.  
- Organization: list, upload (does not auto-activate), activate (up to 10 atomically/idempotently), deactivate (up to 10 atomically/idempotently), get (active or not), modify name, delete (must be inactive for org and all projects).  
- Project: list, activate (up to 10 atomically/idempotently), deactivate (up to 10 atomically/idempotently).

Chat  
Legacy-style chat completions for conversations.  
- Stored chat completions: list those created with `store=true`.  
- Create chat completion: generate responses for text/vision/audio prompts; supports streaming. Some parameters apply only to reasoning models; see reasoning guide for unsupported parameters.  
- Stored item management: get, update metadata, delete stored completions; get messages of stored completion (only for `store=true`).

Completions  
Legacy prompt-completion interface.  
- Create completion: generate one or more continuations for a text prompt; supports streaming list of completion objects.

Conversations  
High-level conversation entities and items.  
- Conversation items: list, create, get, delete items for a conversation.  
- Conversations: create, get, update, delete conversation (deleting does not delete items).

Embeddings  
- Create embedding: generate vector representations of inputs for use in ML systems.

Evals  
Define, run, and manage evaluations of model performance.  
- Evaluations: list, create (specifying grading and data source schema), get, update, delete.  
- Runs: list runs for an evaluation, create a run (data source + model config validated vs eval schema), get, cancel, delete.  
- Outputs: list output items for a run and get an individual output item.  
- See Evals guide for supported graders/datasources.

Files  
Upload and manage files used by various features.  
- Files: list, upload (up to 512 MB per file; 2.5 TB per project; no org-wide limit), retrieve metadata, delete (removes from vector stores), download content.  
- Usage constraints:  
  - Assistants: supports specific file types, up to 2M tokens; see Assistants Tools guide.  
  - Fine-tuning: `.jsonl` only with specific formats for chat or completions.  
  - Batch: `.jsonl` up to 200 MB with specific request format.  
- Contact OpenAI for storage limit increases.

Fine-tuning  
Manage model tuning and related permissions/graders.  
- Graders: run and validate grader configs.  
- Checkpoint permissions (admin API key required): list, create (share checkpoints with other projects), delete.  
- Jobs: list paginated fine-tuning jobs; create job from dataset (response includes job status and resulting model name); retrieve; cancel immediately; list checkpoints; list events (status updates); pause; resume.  
- See model optimization guide for details.

Groups and Roles (Organization Level)  
Manage groups, group membership, and org-level roles.  
- Group role assignments: list roles for a group, assign org role to group, unassign.  
- Group users: list users in a group, add user, remove user.  
- Groups: list, create, update, delete groups.  
- Roles (org): list roles, create custom role, update, delete.

Images  
Image generation and editing.  
- Create image: generate an image from a prompt.  
- Image edits: edit/extend images using source images + prompt; supports `gpt-image-1.5`, `gpt-image-1`, `gpt-image-1-mini`, `chatgpt-image-latest`, and `dall-e-2`.  
- Variations: create variations of an input image (supported only for `dall-e-2`).

Invites  
Manage invitations to organizations.  
- Invites: list, create invite for user (must accept before having access), retrieve, delete (cannot delete once accepted).

Models  
Inspection and lifecycle of models.  
- List models with owner and availability.  
- Retrieve model with owner and permission info.  
- Delete fine-tuned models (Owner role required).

Moderations  
Safety classification for text and/or images.  
- Create moderation: detect potentially harmful content; see moderation guide.

Projects, Project Access, and Project Roles  
Manage projects, their users, groups, roles, API keys, rate limits, and service accounts.  

Project-role assignments and access:  
- Project group roles: list, assign, unassign roles for groups within a project.  
- Project groups: list groups with access, grant/revoke group access to a project.  
- Project user roles: list, assign, unassign roles for users within a project.

Projects:  
- Projects: list, create (cannot be deleted, only archived), retrieve, modify.  
- API keys: list project API keys, retrieve, delete (returns confirmation or error if key is for a service account).  
- Archiving: archive a project (cannot be used or updated afterward).  
- Rate limits: list and update per-model project rate limits.  
- Service accounts: list, create (returns unredacted key), retrieve, delete (fails if project is archived).  
- Users: list users in project, add existing org users, retrieve, modify roles, delete (fails if project archived).

Realtime  
Realtime interactions, especially over WebRTC and SIP.  
- WebRTC/SIP calls: create Realtime call (returns SDP answer), accept incoming SIP calls with configured session, hang up active calls, transfer (REFER) active SIP calls, reject incoming calls with SIP status code.  
- Client auth: create Realtime client secrets with TTL and session configuration for browser/mobile apps; returns secret and effective session.  
- Realtime sessions: create ephemeral API tokens for client-side use with session parameters; returns session and `client_secret`.  
- Realtime transcription sessions: analogous ephemeral tokens specifically for realtime transcription sessions, with `transcription_session` configuration.

Responses  
New unified interface recommended over Chat Completions.  
- Create response: generate model responses from text/image inputs; supports text and JSON outputs, function-calling for custom code, and built-in tools (web search, file search, etc.).  
- Manage responses: get by ID, delete, cancel (only responses created with `background=true`; see background guide).  
- Input items: list input items associated with a given response.

Project Roles (Per Project)  
- Project roles: list roles for a project, create custom project role, update, delete project roles.

Skills  
Manage "skills" bundles for the current project.  
- Skills: list all skills; create; get by ID; update default version pointer; delete.  
- Skill content and versions: download skill bundle zip; list versions; create new immutable version; get a specific version.

nodes.name: llm
nodes.output:
GitHub’s v3 REST API (v1.1.4, `https://api.github.com`) exposes 1099 endpoints. The excerpt focuses on the `actions` domain, which manages GitHub Actions at the enterprise, organization, and repository levels. For detailed docs per endpoint, use `https://oapis.org/summary/github/[idOrRoute]`.

High‑level capabilities covered:

- Manage global GitHub Actions configuration (permissions, allowed actions, workflow access, default permissions, artifact/log retention).
- Control Actions caching limits and usage across enterprises, organizations, and repositories.
- Fully manage runners (GitHub‑hosted and self‑hosted), including groups, images, machine specs, registration/removal tokens, labels, and just‑in‑time configuration.
- Manage Actions secrets and variables at organization and repository scope, including scoping to selected repositories.
- Manage workflow runs, jobs, logs, artifacts, approvals, custom gates, and deployment reviews.

Key functional areas and representative endpoints:

1) Enterprise‑level cache and storage limits
- Get and set Actions cache retention limit for an enterprise.
- Get and set Actions cache storage limit for an enterprise.

2) Organization‑level cache, storage, and usage
- Get and set Actions cache retention limit for an organization.
- Get and set Actions cache storage limit for an organization.
- Get actions cache usage for an organization and list repositories with cache usage.

3) Organization‑level GitHub‑hosted runners and images
- List and create GitHub‑hosted runners for an organization; get, update, and delete a specific hosted runner.
- List and manage custom images for GitHub‑hosted runners:
  - List custom images and their versions.
  - Get and delete a custom image or a specific image version.
- Discover GitHub‑owned and partner images available for an organization.
- Retrieve limits, machine specs, and supported platforms for GitHub‑hosted runners.

4) Organization‑level Actions policies and permissions
- Get and set:
  - GitHub Actions permissions for an organization.
  - Artifact and log retention settings.
  - Fork‑PR contributor approval requirements.
  - Private repo fork PR workflow settings.
  - Allowed actions and reusable workflows.
  - Self‑hosted runner settings.
  - Default workflow permissions.
- Manage the set of repositories that:
  - Are enabled for GitHub Actions.
  - Are allowed to use self‑hosted runners.
  - Use particular org‑scoped secrets or variables.

5) Organization‑level self‑hosted runners and groups
- Runner groups:
  - List, create, get, update, and delete self‑hosted runner groups for an organization.
  - List GitHub‑hosted runners in a group.
  - Manage repository access to a runner group (list, set, add, remove).
  - Manage which self‑hosted runners belong to a group (list, set, add, remove).
- Runners:
  - List self‑hosted runners for an organization.
  - List runner applications for an organization.
  - Generate just‑in‑time runner configuration.
  - Create registration and remove tokens.
  - Get and delete a specific self‑hosted runner.
  - Manage runner labels (list, add custom labels, set full label set, remove all custom labels, remove individual custom labels).

6) Organization‑level secrets and variables
- Secrets:
  - List organization secrets.
  - Get the organization’s public key for encrypting secrets.
  - Get, create/update, and delete an organization secret.
  - Manage repository selection for each org secret (list, set, add repo, remove repo).
- Variables:
  - List, create, get, update, and delete organization variables.
  - Manage selected repositories for each org variable (list, set, add repo, remove repo).

7) Repository‑level artifacts and cache
- Artifacts:
  - List artifacts for a repository.
  - Get, delete, and download an artifact.
- Cache:
  - Get and set Actions cache retention limit for a repository.
  - Get and set Actions cache storage limit for a repository.
  - Get cache usage for a repository.
  - List caches in a repository.
  - Delete caches by cache key or by cache ID.

8) Jobs and workflow runs
- Jobs:
  - Get a job for a workflow run.
  - Download job logs for a workflow run.
  - Re‑run a job from a workflow run.
- Workflow runs:
  - List workflow runs for a repository.
  - Get and delete a specific workflow run.
  - Get review history for a run and approve a workflow run (for fork PRs).
  - List workflow run artifacts.
  - Get a specific workflow run attempt and list its jobs.
  - Download logs for a workflow run attempt or for the entire workflow run.
  - Delete workflow run logs.
  - Cancel or force‑cancel a workflow run.
  - Re‑run a workflow or re‑run only failed jobs.
  - Review custom deployment protection rules (custom gates).
  - Get pending deployments for a run and review them.
  - Get workflow run usage.

9) Repository‑level OIDC, permissions, and workflow access
- OIDC:
  - Get and set the customization template for an OIDC subject claim for a repository.
- Permissions and workflow access:
  - Get and set Actions permissions for a repository.
  - Get and set the level of access for workflows outside the repository.
  - Get and set artifact and log retention settings.
  - Get and set fork‑PR contributor approval permissions.
  - Get and set private repo fork PR workflow settings.
  - Get and set allowed actions and reusable workflows.
  - Get and set default workflow permissions.

10) Repository‑level runners
- List self‑hosted runners for a repository.
- List runner applications for a repository.
- Generate just‑in‑time runner configuration for a repository.
- Create registration and remove tokens for a repository.
- Get and delete a specific self‑hosted runner from a repository.
- Manage runner labels at repo scope (list, add custom labels, set full label set, remove all custom labels, remove a specific custom label).

11) Repository‑level secrets and variables (plus org‑scoped visibility)
- Repository secrets:
  - List repository secrets.
  - Get the repository’s public key.
  - Get, create/update, and delete a repository secret.
- Repository variables:
  - List repository variables.
  - Create a repository variable.
- Additionally:
  - List repository organization secrets (org secrets visible to this repo).
  - List repository organization variables (org variables visible to this repo).

All endpoints follow the pattern of listing, getting, creating/updating, deleting, or configuring resources related to GitHub Actions workflows, runner infrastructure, security (secrets/OIDC), execution environment (runners, images, cache), and governance (permissions, approvals, retention, allowed actions) across enterprise, organization, and repository scopes.

nodes.output:
OpenAI API v2.3.0 (https://api.openai.com) is a REST API with 241 endpoints. Full reference: https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference and endpoint summaries: https://oapis.org/summary/openai/[idOrRoute].

Assistants  
Build assistants that use models and tools around the concepts of assistants, threads, messages, runs, and run steps.  
- Assistants: create, list, get, modify, delete.  
- Threads: create, get, modify, delete; create a thread and run in one call.  
- Messages: list, create, get, modify, delete within a thread.  
- Runs: list, create, get, modify, cancel; manage run steps and retrieve steps.  
- Tool outputs: when a run requires tool outputs, submit all tool call outputs in one request.

Audio  
Convert between text and audio and manage voice consent/voices.  
- Text to speech: generate audio from text (`createSpeech`), returning a file or audio stream.  
- Speech to text: transcribe audio (`createTranscription`) to JSON, diarized JSON, or verbose JSON, with streaming support.  
- Translation: translate audio into English (`createTranslation`).  
- Voice consents: list, upload, get, update (metadata), delete.  
- Custom voices: create voice definitions.

Audit Logs  
- List actions and configuration changes in an organization (`list-audit-logs`).

Batch  
Run large batches of API requests asynchronously.  
- Batches: list, create (from uploaded file), retrieve, cancel in‑progress batches (transitioning to `cancelled` with partial results).

Certificates  
Manage TLS or similar certificates at organization and project level.  
- Organization: list, upload (does not auto‑activate), activate/deactivate up to 10 at a time (atomic/idempotent), get, modify name, delete (only if inactive everywhere).  
- Project: list, activate/deactivate up to 10 at a time (atomic/idempotent).

Chat Completions  
Legacy-style chat interface; new projects are encouraged to use Responses.  
- List stored chat completions (only those created with `store=true`).  
- Create chat completion: generate responses for chat, vision, or audio; supports streaming; some parameters differ for reasoning models.  
- Get/update/delete stored chat completions (metadata-only updates) and fetch their messages.

Completions  
Text-completion style interface.  
- Create completion: generate one or more completions from a prompt; optional streaming.

Conversations  
High-level conversation entities and items.  
- Items: list, create, get, delete items by conversation and item ID.  
- Conversations: create, get, update, delete (deleting does not delete items).

Embeddings  
Vector representations of text.  
- Create embedding: generate embedding vectors for input text.

Evals  
Platform for evaluating model quality.  
- Evaluations: list, create (define config, data source, and graders), get, update, delete.  
- Runs: list runs for eval, create new run (validate datasource schema), get, cancel, delete.  
- Run outputs: list output items and get a single output item.

Files  
Upload and manage files used by multiple features.  
- Files: list, upload (up to 512 MB each; project total up to 2.5 TB; no org-wide limit), retrieve metadata, delete (also removes from vector stores), download contents.  
- Notes: Assistants support up to 2M tokens and specific file types; Fine-tuning uses `.jsonl` with specific schemas; Batch uses `.jsonl` up to 200 MB with a specific format; limits can be increased via support.

Fine-tuning  
Manage fine-tuning jobs and grader utilities.  
- Graders: run and validate custom graders.  
- Checkpoint permissions (admin key required): list all permissions for a fine-tuned checkpoint; create and delete permissions to share fine-tuned models across projects.  
- Jobs: list paginated fine-tuning jobs, create job (returns status and final model name), retrieve job, cancel immediately, list checkpoints, list events, pause, and resume jobs.

Groups and Roles (Org Level)  
Group org roles: list role assignments for a group; assign/unassign organization roles for groups.  
Group users: list users in a group; add/remove users from a group.  
Groups: list, create, update, delete groups.  
Roles: list/create/update/delete organization roles; also list/create/update/delete project roles for a project.

Images  
Image generation and editing.  
- Create image: generate an image from a prompt (supports GPT Image models, etc).  
- Edit image: edit/extend images using source images + prompt, for GPT Image and DALL·E 2-capable models.  
- Variations: create variations of an existing image (DALL·E 2 only).

Invites  
Organization invitations.  
- Invites: list, create (invite user to org), retrieve, delete (only if not already accepted).

Models  
Model catalogue.  
- List models with basic info such as owner and availability.  
- Retrieve model details including owner and permissions.  
- Delete fine-tuned models (Owner role required).

Moderations  
Safety classification for text and images.  
- Create moderation: classify if inputs are potentially harmful; see moderation guide for details.

Projects & Access Control  
Project-level grouping of usage, keys, and access.

Project group role assignments  
- List project roles assigned to a group; assign/unassign project roles for groups.

Project groups  
- List groups with access to a project; add group (grant access); remove group (revoke access).

Project user role assignments  
- List project roles assigned to a user; assign/unassign project roles for users.

Projects  
- Projects: list, create (cannot be deleted, only archived), retrieve, modify.  
- API keys: list, retrieve, delete (error if it’s a service-account key).  
- Archive project: archived projects cannot be used or updated.  
- Rate limits: list and update per model.  
- Service accounts: list, create (returns unredacted key), retrieve, delete (not allowed if project archived).  
- Users: list project users, add (must already be org members), retrieve, modify role, delete (not allowed if project archived).

Realtime  
Endpoints to manage realtime WebRTC/SIP sessions and ephemeral credentials.  
- Calls: create WebRTC call and get SDP answer; accept SIP call; hang up active calls; transfer SIP call using REFER; reject calls with SIP status.  
- Client secrets: create short-lived Realtime client secrets with session configuration; used to authenticate clients without exposing main API key.  
- Sessions: create ephemeral session tokens and associated session objects for Realtime API and for Realtime transcription, each returning a `client_secret` ephemeral key.

Responses  
Recommended unified interface for generating model outputs and using tools.  
- Create response: generate text or JSON from text/image inputs; supports custom code, tools (web search, file search), and background responses.  
- Manage responses: get, delete, cancel (only for responses created with `background=true`).  
- Input items: list input items associated with a response.

Skills  
Manage reusable skills (deployable capability bundles).  
- List skills for the current project.  
- Create skill, get by ID, update default version, delete.  
- Skill content: download skill zip bundle.  
- Skill versions: list versions for a skill, create immutable version, get version by ID.

nodes.name: llm
nodes.output:
GitHub’s v3 REST API (v1.1.4, base `https://api.github.com`) exposes 1099 endpoints. The excerpt focuses on the `actions` namespace, which provides REST endpoints to manage GitHub Actions at enterprise, organization, and repository scope, covering caches, storage, runners, permissions, workflows, secrets, variables, and artifacts. For full endpoint details there is a per-endpoint summary URL pattern: `https://oapis.org/summary/github/[idOrRoute]`.

Below is the condensed structure of the `actions` API as described in the content.

GitHub Actions – Enterprise-Level Cache & Storage Limits
- Get/set Actions cache retention limit for an enterprise.
- Get/set Actions cache storage limit for an enterprise.

Organization-Level Cache, Storage, Runners, Permissions, Secrets, and Variables
- Cache & storage for organizations:
  - Get/set GitHub Actions cache retention limit for an organization.
  - Get/set GitHub Actions cache storage limit for an organization.
  - Get overall Actions cache usage for an organization.
  - List repositories along with their Actions cache usage.
- GitHub-hosted runners for organizations:
  - List GitHub-hosted runners at org level.
  - Create, get, update, and delete a GitHub-hosted runner for an org.
  - List custom images for an org; get and delete a custom image; list/get/delete specific image versions.
  - Discover GitHub-owned images and partner images for GitHub-hosted runners.
  - Get runner limits (quotas), machine specs, and supported platforms for GitHub-hosted runners in an org.
- Self-hosted runner groups and runners (org scope):
  - List/create/get/update/delete self-hosted runner groups for an org.
  - For a runner group:
    - List GitHub-hosted runners in the group.
    - List/set/add/remove repository access to the group.
    - List/set/add/remove self-hosted runners in the group.
  - Organization-level self-hosted runners:
    - List self-hosted runners for an org.
    - List runner applications (binaries) for an org.
    - Generate just-in-time (JIT) runner configuration for an org.
    - Create registration and removal tokens for runners at org level.
    - Get or delete an individual self-hosted runner.
    - Manage runner labels: list labels; add/set/remove custom labels; remove all custom labels.
- Organization-wide Actions permissions and policies:
  - Get/set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization (e.g., enabling/disabling Actions).
  - Get/set artifact and log retention settings.
  - Get/set fork PR contributor approval requirements.
  - Get/set private-repo fork PR workflows settings.
  - Manage which repositories are enabled for Actions in an org:
    - List repos explicitly enabled for Actions.
    - Set the full list of enabled repos.
    - Enable/disable a particular repo.
  - Get/set allowed actions and reusable workflows for an org (e.g., limit to local, GitHub-verified, or selected).
  - Self-hosted runner usage controls:
    - Get/set self-hosted runners settings for an org.
    - List/set/add/remove repositories allowed to use self-hosted runners.
  - Default workflow permissions:
    - Get/set default workflow permissions for the organization.
- Organization-level secrets:
  - List organization secrets.
  - Get the org’s public key (for encrypting secrets).
  - Get/create/update/delete a specific org secret.
  - Manage repository access to org secrets:
    - List selected repositories associated with an org secret.
    - Set the full list of selected repos.
    - Add or remove a specific repo from an org secret.
- Organization-level variables:
  - List/create/get/update/delete organization variables.
  - Manage repository access to org variables:
    - List selected repositories that can use an org variable.
    - Set the full repo list.
    - Add or remove a specific repo for an org variable.

Repository-Level Artifacts, Cache, Workflows, Permissions, Secrets, and Variables
- Artifacts for a repository:
  - List artifacts in a repository.
  - Get a specific artifact.
  - Delete an artifact.
  - Download an artifact.
- Cache limits, storage, and usage for a repository:
  - Get/set Actions cache retention limit for a repository.
  - Get/set Actions cache storage limit for a repository.
  - Get cache usage for a repository.
  - List caches for a repository.
  - Delete caches by cache key.
  - Delete a cache by cache ID.
- Jobs and workflow runs:
  - For a workflow run:
    - Get details of a specific job.
    - Download job logs.
    - Re-run a single job.
  - Workflow runs per repository:
    - List workflow runs for a repository.
    - Get a specific workflow run.
    - Delete a workflow run.
    - Get review history for a workflow run.
    - Approve a workflow run for forked pull requests.
    - List artifacts produced by a workflow run.
    - Get a specific workflow run attempt and list its jobs.
    - Download logs for a workflow run attempt.
    - Cancel or force-cancel a workflow run.
    - Review custom deployment protection rules (“custom gates”) for a run.
    - List jobs for a workflow run.
    - Download workflow run logs and delete them.
    - Get pending deployments for a workflow run and review/approve them.
    - Re-run workflows entirely or re-run only failed jobs.
    - Get workflow run usage (e.g., compute time).
- Repository-level external workflow access:
  - Get/set the level of access that workflows outside the repository have to run in it.
- Repository-level Actions permissions and policies:
  - Get/set Actions permissions for an individual repository.
  - Get/set artifact and log retention settings for a repository.
  - Get/set fork PR contributor approval permissions for a repository.
  - Get/set private-repo fork PR workflows settings at repository level.
  - Get/set allowed actions and reusable workflows for a repository.
  - Get/set default workflow permissions for a repository.
- Repo-level self-hosted runners:
  - List self-hosted runners configured for a repository.
  - List runner applications for a repository.
  - Generate JIT runner configuration for a repository.
  - Create registration and removal tokens at repo scope.
  - Get or delete a specific self-hosted runner from the repository.
  - Manage runner labels: list labels; add/set/remove custom labels; remove all custom labels for that repo’s runner.
- OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim customization:
  - Get/set the OIDC subject claim customization template for a repository (for workload identity federation with cloud providers).
- Repository view of org-level configuration:
  - List repository organization secrets available to the repo.
  - List repository organization variables available to the repo.
- Repository-level secrets:
  - List repository secrets.
  - Get the repo’s public key (for encrypting repo secrets).
  - Get/create/update/delete a repository secret.
- Repository-level variables:
  - List repository variables.
  - Create repository variables (the content truncates at this point but implies standard CRUD for repo variables).

nodes.output:
OpenAI provides a large REST API (v2.3.0, 241 endpoints) at `https://api.openai.com`, with detailed reference at `https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference`. For per-endpoint docs via oapis.org, use `https://oapis.org/summary/openai/[idOrRoute]`.

Key functional areas and capabilities:

Assistants  
- Full lifecycle for AI assistants, threads, messages, runs, and run steps.  
- Create, list, retrieve, modify, and delete assistants.  
- Create threads (conversations), or create-and-run in one call.  
- Manage threads: get, modify, delete.  
- Manage messages in a thread: list, create, get, modify, delete.  
- Manage runs on a thread: list, create, get, modify, cancel (if in progress).  
- Manage run steps: list steps for a run, get a step.  
- Submit tool outputs for runs that require tool output, in a single request.

Audio  
- Text-to-speech: generate audio from text (`createSpeech`), return file or streamed audio events.  
- Speech-to-text: transcribe audio into its source language (`createTranscription`), returned as JSON/diarized JSON/verbose JSON or streamed events.  
- Audio translation: translate audio to English (`createTranslation`).  
- Voice consents: list, upload, retrieve, update metadata, and delete voice consent recordings.  
- Custom voices: create a custom voice (`createVoice`).

Audit Logs  
- List user actions and configuration changes across the organization.

Batch  
- Asynchronous large-scale request execution.  
- List batches, create/execute a batch from an uploaded request file, retrieve batches, cancel in-progress batches (with partial results retained in output file once cancelled).

Certificates  
- Organization-level certificate management: list, upload (without auto-activation), activate, deactivate (up to 10 per call, atomically and idempotently).  
- Retrieve and modify certificate metadata (name only), and delete certificates (must be inactive everywhere).  
- Project-level certificate management: list, activate, deactivate (same atomic/idempotent constraints).

Chat  
- Chat Completions (legacy-style conversational endpoint).  
- List stored chat completions (`store=true`), create new chat completion responses (supporting text, vision, audio scenarios), get/update/delete stored completions, and list messages from stored completions.  
- OpenAI recommends using the newer Responses API for new projects and refers to guides on text, vision, audio, and reasoning model parameter support.

Completions  
- Classic text completions based on a prompt.  
- Create completion for given prompts, optionally streamed, returning completion objects.

Conversations  
- Generic conversation management separate from Assistants.  
- Manage conversation items: list, create, get, delete items by conversation and item ID.  
- Manage conversations: create, get, update, delete (deleting a conversation does not delete items).

Embeddings  
- Create vector embeddings from input text (`createEmbedding`) for ML and similarity tasks.

Evals  
- Platform evaluation management and execution for model performance testing.  
- List, create, get, update, delete eval definitions.  
- Manage eval runs: list runs per eval, create/run with specified data source and model config (data validated against eval schema), get run, cancel run, delete run.  
- Inspect outputs: list run output items and retrieve specific output items.  
- Documentation via the Evals guide.

Files  
- Central file storage for Assistants, Fine-tuning, Batch, etc.  
- List, upload (up to 512 MB per file, 2.5 TB per project), retrieve metadata, delete, and download files.  
- Per-feature constraints:  
  - Assistants: supports specific file types up to ~2M tokens (see Assistants Tools guide).  
  - Fine-tuning: `.jsonl` only, with specific formats for chat/completion fine-tuning.  
  - Batch: `.jsonl` up to 200 MB with required format.  
- Storage limit increases available via support.

Fine-tuning  
- Graders: run and validate graders that score model outputs.  
- Fine-tuned checkpoint permissions (admin-only endpoints): list, create, delete permissions for sharing fine-tuned checkpoints across projects.  
- Fine-tuning jobs: list paginated jobs, create a new fine-tuning job (with status and resulting model name), retrieve details, cancel, pause, resume.  
- Checkpoints and events: list checkpoints for a job and list status/events for a job.  
- Documentation links to model optimization guides.

Groups and Access Control (Org-Level)  
- Group organization role assignments: list roles assigned to a group, assign/unassign org roles for groups.  
- Group users: list users in a group, add a user to a group, remove a user from a group.  
- Groups: list org groups, create/update/delete groups.

Images  
- Image generation and editing.  
- Create images from prompts using supported models (`gpt-image-*`, `chatgpt-image-latest`, `dall-e-2`).  
- Edit or extend existing images given source images and a prompt.  
- Create variations of an image (currently `dall-e-2` only).

Invites  
- Organization invitations: list invites, create invites for users, retrieve and delete invites (cannot delete an invite once accepted).

Models  
- Model catalog management.  
- List available models with basic metadata (owner, availability).  
- Retrieve a single model’s details.  
- Delete fine-tuned models (requires org Owner role).

Moderations  
- Safety classification for text and/or image inputs.  
- Create moderation requests to detect potentially harmful content, with guidance in the moderation docs.

Project-Level Access Control  
- Project group role assignments: list project roles for a group, assign/unassign roles to a group in a project.  
- Project groups: list groups with access to a project, grant/revoke group access.  
- Project user role assignments: list project roles for a user, assign/unassign roles to a user.

Projects  
- Core project management: list, create (cannot be deleted; only archived), retrieve, modify, archive.  
- Project API keys: list, retrieve, delete keys (error if key is a service account key on deletion).  
- Project rate limits: list and update per-model rate limits.  
- Service accounts: list, create (returns unredacted API key), retrieve, delete (fails if project is archived).  
- Project users: list users, add existing org members, retrieve, modify roles, and delete users (cannot manage users for archived projects).

Realtime  
- Realtime API over WebRTC/SIP for live interactions and transcriptions.  
- Create WebRTC realtime calls, accept incoming SIP calls, hang up active calls, transfer calls via SIP REFER, and reject calls with SIP status codes.  
- Create realtime client secrets with session configs (short-lived tokens for clients without exposing main API key).  
- Create realtime sessions and transcription sessions that return an ephemeral client_secret usable in browser/mobile apps, with configurable session parameters.

Responses  
- Modern unified endpoint for model responses (recommended vs Chat Completions).  
- Create responses from text or image inputs with text or JSON outputs; supports custom code tools and built-in tools such as web search and file search.  
- Manage responses: get, delete, cancel (for background responses created with `background=true`).  
- Inspect inputs: list input items associated with a response.

Roles  
- Role management at org and project level.  
- Organization roles: list, create custom roles, update, delete custom roles.  
- Project roles: list, create custom roles, update, delete custom roles.

Skills  
- Skill management (modular units of functionality).  
- List all skills for the current project, create a skill, get details, update the default version pointer, delete a skill, and download skill content as a zip bundle.  
- Versioning: list versions for a skill, create new immutable versions, and retrieve a specific version.

GitHub provides a large v3 REST API (version 1.1.4, base URL `https://api.github.com`) with 1099 endpoints. The excerpt describes the `actions` section, which exposes endpoints to manage GitHub Actions at enterprise, organization, and repository levels, including configuration, runners, permissions, caches, artifacts, secrets, variables, and workflows. For detailed docs per endpoint, you can visit `https://oapis.org/summary/github/[idOrRoute]`.

Key capability areas in the `actions` group:

- Enterprise-level cache configuration:
  - Get and set GitHub Actions cache retention limits for an enterprise.
  - Get and set GitHub Actions cache storage limits for an enterprise.

- Organization-level cache configuration and usage:
  - Get and set cache retention limits for an organization.
  - Get and set cache storage limits for an organization.
  - Get cache usage for an organization.
  - List repositories and their cache usage within an organization.

- GitHub-hosted runners for organizations:
  - List and create GitHub-hosted runners for an organization.
  - Manage custom images for hosted runners:
    - List, get, delete custom images.
    - List, get, delete specific image versions.
  - Inspect available images:
    - Get GitHub-owned images for hosted runners.
    - Get partner images for hosted runners.
  - Discover capabilities:
    - Get limits, machine specs, and supported platforms for hosted runners.
  - Manage individual GitHub-hosted runners:
    - Get, update, and delete a specific hosted runner.

- Organization-level Actions settings and permissions:
  - Get and set GitHub Actions permissions at the organization level.
  - Get and set artifact and log retention settings.
  - Get and set fork pull-request contributor approval requirements.
  - Get and set private repository fork PR workflow settings.
  - Manage which repositories are allowed to use Actions within the organization:

    - Set the full list of selected repositories.
    - Enable or disable an individual repository for Actions.
  - Manage allowed actions and reusable workflows at the org level:

  - Self-hosted runners policies:

    - Manage which repositories may use self-hosted runners:
      - List, set, add, and remove allowed repositories.
  - Default workflow permissions:
    - Get and set default workflow permissions for the organization.

- Self-hosted runner groups at org level:
  - List, create, get, update, and delete self-hosted runner groups.
  - Manage GitHub-hosted runners in a group:

  - Manage repository access to a runner group:

  - Manage self-hosted runners in a group:
    - List, set, add, and remove self-hosted runners assigned to a group.

- Self-hosted runners at org level (outside groups):

  - List runner applications (binaries used to configure runners).
  - Generate just-in-time runner configuration for an organization.
  - Create registration and removal tokens for runner registration/deletion.
  - Get and delete a specific self-hosted runner.
  - Manage labels on self-hosted runners:

- Organization secrets and variables for Actions:
  - Secrets:

    - Get the organization public key used to encrypt secret values.
    - Get, create/update, and delete individual org secrets.
    - Manage repository access to an org secret:
      - List selected repositories, set the set of repositories, add or remove a repository.
  - Variables:
    - List, create, get, update, and delete organization-level variables.
    - Manage repository access to an org variable:
      - List selected repositories, set the full list, add or remove a repository.

- Repository artifacts and cache configuration:
  - Artifacts:
    - List artifacts for a repository.
    - Get, delete, and download an individual artifact.
  - Repository-level cache settings and usage:
    - Get and set cache retention limits for a repository.
    - Get and set cache storage limits for a repository.
    - Get cache usage for a repository.
    - List caches for a repository.
    - Delete caches by cache key or by cache ID.

- Workflow jobs and logs:
  - Get details of a job for a workflow run.
  - Download job logs for a workflow run.
  - Re-run a specific job from a workflow run.

- Repository OIDC configuration:
  - Get and set the customization template for the OIDC subject (`sub`) claim for a repository (for identity federation).

- Repository view of organization-level configuration:
  - List organization-level secrets available to a repository.
  - List organization-level variables available to a repository.
  - Get and set Actions permissions for a repository.
  - Get and set the level of access for workflows outside of the repository.
  - Get and set repository-level artifact and log retention.
  - Get and set fork PR contributor approval settings at repository level.
  - Get and set private repo fork PR workflows settings at repository level.
  - Get and set which actions and reusable workflows are allowed within the repository.
  - Get and set default workflow permissions for the repository.

- Self-hosted runners at repository level:
  - List self-hosted runners assigned to a repository.
  - List runner applications for a repository.
  - Generate just-in-time runner configuration for a repository.
  - Create registration and removal tokens for repository-scoped runners.
  - Get and delete a specific repository self-hosted runner.
  - Manage labels on repository-level self-hosted runners:
    - List labels; add, set (replace), remove all, or remove specific custom labels.

- Workflow runs, artifacts, approvals, and usage:
  - List workflow runs for a repository.
  - Get and delete a specific workflow run.
  - Retrieve review history for a workflow run (e.g., approvals).
  - Approve a workflow run for a fork pull request.

- Repository secrets and variables:

nodes.name: llm
llm
nodes.output:
OpenAI’s REST API v2.3.0 (https://api.openai.com) exposes 241 endpoints across many feature areas. Detailed per-endpoint docs: `https://oapis.org/summary/openai/[idOrRoute]` and https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference.

ASSISTANTS  
Build multi-step assistants with tools and threads.  
- Assistants: list, create (with model+instructions), get, modify, delete.  
- Threads: create, get, modify, delete; create a thread and immediately run it.  
- Messages: list, create, get, modify, delete messages in a thread.  
- Runs: list, create, get, modify, cancel runs for a thread.  
- Run steps: list and get steps for a run.  
- Submit tool outputs: when a run requires tool outputs, submit all outputs in one request.

AUDIO  
Convert between text and audio; manage voice consents and custom voices.  
- Text-to-speech: generate audio from text (`createSpeech`), returning file content or audio stream.  
- Transcription: transcribe audio in its input language (`createTranscription`), returning JSON/verbose/diarized JSON or streamed events.  
- Translation: translate audio to English (`createTranslation`).  
- Voice consents: list, upload, get, update metadata, delete voice consent recordings.  
- Voices: create custom voices.

AUDIT LOGS  
- List organization-level user actions and configuration changes.

BATCH  
Run large numbers of API requests asynchronously.  
- Batches: list, create (from uploaded JSONL file), retrieve, cancel.  
- Cancel moves batch to `cancelling` and then `cancelled`, with partial results in an output file.

CERTIFICATES  
Manage TLS-like certificates at organization and project levels.  
- Organization: list certificates; upload (does not auto-activate); activate/deactivate (up to 10 per request, atomic and idempotent); get; modify name; delete (only if inactive everywhere).  
- Project: list certificates; activate/deactivate (up to 10 per request, atomic and idempotent).

CHAT  
Legacy-style chat completions (new projects should prefer Responses).  
- Stored chat completions: list (only those created with `store: true`); get; update metadata; delete; list underlying messages.  
- Create chat completion: model response to a message list; supports text, vision, audio use cases; some parameters are specific to reasoning models. Supports normal and streaming responses.

COMPLETIONS  
Classic text completion endpoint.  
- Create completion from a prompt; supports streaming; returns completion object(s).

CONVERSATIONS  
Structured conversation management separate from Chat Completions.  
- Items: list, create, get, delete items in a conversation.  
- Conversations: create, get, update, delete (deletion does not remove items).

EMBEDDINGS  
- Create embedding vectors for inputs (`createEmbedding`) for ML/search use.

EVALS  
Configure and run model evaluations.  
- Evaluations: list; create (define test criteria and data-source config); get; update; delete.  
- Runs: list runs for an evaluation; create run with data source and model config; get, cancel, delete run.  
- Outputs: list output items for a run; get individual output items.

FILES  
Upload and manage files used by Assistants, Fine-tuning, Batch, etc.  
- Files: list, upload (up to 512 MB per file, 2.5 TB per project; orgs have no global limit), retrieve metadata, delete, download.  
- Assistants support large token files and specific types (see Assistants tools docs).  
- Fine-tuning only supports `.jsonl` with specific formats.  
- Batch only supports `.jsonl` up to 200 MB with a specific format.  
- Contact OpenAI support to raise storage limits.

FINE-TUNING  
Manage fine-tune jobs and permissions.  
- Graders: run and validate graders.  
- Checkpoint permissions (admin API key required): list all permissions for a fine-tuned checkpoint; create permission to share with other projects; delete permission.  
- Jobs: list paginated jobs; create fine-tuning job (returns job status and eventual model name); retrieve job; cancel; list checkpoints; list events; pause; resume.

GROUPS AND ACCESS CONTROL  
Organization-level group roles:  
- List role assignments for a group; assign or unassign org-level roles.  

Group users:  
- List users in a group; add or remove users from a group.

Groups:  
- List groups; create, update, delete groups.

PROJECT SCOPED ACCESS  
Project group role assignments:  
- List project roles for a group; assign/unassign project roles.  

Project groups:  
- List groups with access to a project; grant or revoke group access.

Project user role assignments:  
- List project roles for a user; assign/unassign user roles in a project.

PROJECTS  
Manage projects and their keys, rate limits, service accounts, and users.  
- Projects: list; create (cannot be deleted, only archived); retrieve; modify; archive (archived projects cannot be used or updated).  
- API keys: list project keys; retrieve; delete (returns confirmation or error if key is for a service account).  
- Rate limits: list per-model limits; update limits.  
- Service accounts: list; create (returns unredacted API key); retrieve; delete (fails if project is archived).  
- Project users: list; add user (must already be org member); retrieve; modify role; delete (fails if project is archived).

IMAGES  
Image generation and editing.  
- Create image from prompt (`createImage`) with GPT Image models and DALL·E-based models.  
- Edit/extend image (`createImageEdit`) with prompt and source images (supports `gpt-image-1.5`, `gpt-image-1`, `gpt-image-1-mini`, `chatgpt-image-latest`, `dall-e-2`).  
- Create variations from an existing image (`createImageVariation`, DALL·E 2 only).

INVITES  
Organization membership invites.  
- List invites; create invite for a user; retrieve invite; delete invite (only if not yet accepted).

MODELS  
List and manage models, including fine-tuned ones.  
- List available models; retrieve a specific model’s info and permissions; delete a fine-tuned model (Owner role required).

MODERATIONS  
Content safety classification for text and/or images.  
- Create moderation (`createModeration`); returns harm categories per moderation guide.

REALTIME  
Real-time API for calls, SIP/WebRTC, and ephemeral auth.  
- Calls: create WebRTC call (returns SDP answer); accept SIP call; hang up; transfer call via SIP REFER; reject SIP call with status code.  
- Client auth: create Realtime client secrets (short-lived tokens with session config) and Realtime sessions/transcription sessions with ephemeral keys for browser or mobile clients; sessions include configurable parameters and return a `client_secret` token.

RESPONSES  
Recommended unified endpoint for new projects (supersedes Chat Completions in many cases).  
- Create a response (`createResponse`): send text/image inputs and get text or JSON outputs; supports tools (custom code, web search, file search), and background responses.  
- Manage responses: get, delete, cancel (only if created with `background: true`).  
- Input items: list input items for a given response.

ROLES  
Define custom roles and permissions.  
- Organization roles: list, create, update, delete custom org roles.  
- Project roles: list, create, update, delete custom project roles.

SKILLS  
Versioned skills (packaged functionality) for a project.  
- Skills: list all skills; create a skill; get by ID; update the default version; delete by ID.  
- Skill content: download a skill’s zip bundle.  
- Skill versions: list versions for a skill; create new immutable version; get a specific version.

nodes.name: llm
nodes.output:
GitHub’s v3 REST API (v1.1.4, `https://api.github.com`) exposes 1,099 endpoints. The excerpt focuses on the `actions` namespace, which manages GitHub Actions at the enterprise, organization, and repository levels. For detailed per-endpoint docs, each id/route is available at `https://oapis.org/summary/github/[idOrRoute]`.

The `actions` API covers these major functional areas:

1) Cache Limits and Usage (Enterprise, Org, Repo)
- Enterprise-level endpoints to get/set:
  - Actions cache retention limit.
  - Actions cache storage limit.
- Organization-level endpoints to get/set:
  - Cache retention limit.
  - Cache storage limit.
  - Cache usage for the org.
  - Per-repository cache usage within the org.
- Repository-level endpoints to:
  - Get/set cache retention limit.
  - Get/set cache storage limit.
  - Get current cache usage.
  - List caches.
  - Delete caches by key or by cache ID.

2) GitHub‑Hosted Runners (Org)
- Manage GitHub-hosted runners for an organization:
  - List all hosted runners.
  - Create a hosted runner.
  - Get/update/delete a specific hosted runner.
- Manage runner images:
  - List custom images in an org.
  - Get/delete a specific custom image.
  - List image versions of a custom image.
  - Get/delete a specific image version.
  - Get GitHub-owned images available for hosted runners.
  - Get partner images available for hosted runners.
- Discover environment capabilities:
  - Get hosted runner limits for the org.
  - Get machine specs for hosted runners.
  - Get platforms supported for hosted runners.

3) Self‑Hosted Runners: Groups, Access, and Labels (Org and Repo)
Organization-level:
- Configure global self-hosted runner settings:
  - Get/set self-hosted runner permissions/settings.
- Control which repositories can use self-hosted runners:
  - List/set repositories allowed to use self-hosted runners.
  - Enable or disable a specific repository’s access to self-hosted runners.
- Manage self-hosted runner groups:
  - List/create/get/update/delete runner groups.
  - List GitHub-hosted runners in a group.
  - List/set/add/remove repository access for a runner group.
  - List/set/add/remove self-hosted runners in a group.
- Manage individual self-hosted runners:
  - List all org-level self-hosted runners.
  - List runner applications (installation images/binaries).
  - Generate just-in-time (JIT) runner configuration.
  - Create registration and removal tokens.
  - Get/delete a specific self-hosted runner.
  - Manage runner labels:
    - List labels for a runner.
    - Add custom labels.
    - Replace all custom labels.
    - Remove all custom labels.
    - Remove a single custom label.

Repository-level:
- Manage repo-scoped self-hosted runners:
  - List self-hosted runners for a repository.
  - List runner applications for a repository.
  - Generate JIT runner configuration for a repo.
  - Create registration and removal tokens for a repo.
  - Get/delete a specific self-hosted runner in the repo.
  - Manage labels for that runner (list/add/set/remove-all/remove-one), same pattern as org-level.

4) Permissions, Allowed Actions, and Workflow Access (Org and Repo)
Organization-level configuration:
- Get/set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization.
- Configure artifact and log retention:
  - Get/set organization-level artifact and log retention settings.
- Configure pull request behavior:
  - Get/set fork PR contributor approval permissions.
  - Get/set private repository fork PR workflow settings.
- Scope which repositories can use Actions:
  - List repositories explicitly enabled for Actions.
  - Set the list of enabled repositories.
  - Enable/disable Actions for a specific repository.
- Configure which actions and workflows are allowed:
  - Get/set allowed actions and reusable workflows.
- Configure default workflow permissions:
  - Get/set default workflow permissions for the organization.

Repository-level configuration:
- Get/set GitHub Actions permissions for the repository.
- Control workflow access from outside the repository:
  - Get/set the level of access that external workflows have to the repo.
- Configure artifact and log retention at repo level:
  - Get/set artifact and log retention settings.
- Configure fork PR behavior:
  - Get/set fork PR contributor approval requirements at repo level.
  - Get/set private repo fork PR workflow settings.
- Configure which actions and reusable workflows are allowed for the repo.
- Get/set default workflow permissions for the repository.
- Configure OIDC subject claim customization:
  - Get/set the OIDC subject claim customization template for the repo.

5) Secrets Management (Org and Repo)
Organization secrets:
- List all organization secrets.
- Get the organization’s public key for encrypting secrets.
- Get a specific organization secret.
- Create or update an organization secret.
- Delete an organization secret.
- Manage which repositories can access a secret:
  - List selected repositories using a secret.
  - Set the full list of selected repositories.
  - Add a repository to the secret’s access list.
  - Remove a repository from the secret’s access list.

Repository secrets:
- List repository secrets.
- Get the repository’s public key for encrypting secrets.
- Get a specific repository secret.
- Create or update a repository secret.
- Delete a repository secret.

6) Variables Management (Org and Repo)
Organization variables:
- List organization variables.
- Create an organization variable.
- Get/update/delete a specific organization variable.
- Manage repository access to a variable:
  - List repositories associated with a variable.
  - Set the full list of associated repositories.
  - Add a repository to the variable’s access list.
  - Remove a repository from the variable’s access list.

Repository variables:
- List repository variables.
- Create repository variables.
- (The content truncates mid-line, but follows the same CRUD pattern as org variables: get, update, delete repo-level variables.)

7) Artifacts and Logs (Repo and Workflow Run)
Repository-level artifact operations:
- List artifacts for a repository.
- Get a specific artifact.
- Delete an artifact.
- Download an artifact.

Workflow-run-level logs and artifacts:
- Per workflow run:
  - List artifacts produced by the run.
  - Download logs for the run.
  - Delete logs for the run.
- Per workflow run attempt:
  - Get a specific run attempt.
  - List jobs for that attempt.
  - Download logs for that attempt.
- Per job:
  - Get a specific job in a workflow run.
  - Download job logs.
  - Re-run a specific job from a workflow run.

8) Workflow Runs: Control, Review, Usage
- List workflow runs for a repository.
- Get a specific workflow run.
- Delete a workflow run.
- Cancel a workflow run.
- Force-cancel a workflow run.
- Re-run a workflow run (entire workflow).
- Re-run only failed jobs from a workflow run.
- Get usage metrics for a workflow run (e.g., billable minutes).
- Manage approval and deployment gating:
  - Get review history for a workflow run.
  - Approve workflow runs for fork pull requests.
  - Get pending deployments for a workflow run.
  - Review and approve/deny pending deployments.
  - Review custom deployment protection rules (custom gates) for a run.

9) Repository-Level Aggregation of Org Configuration
- For a given repository, read organization-scoped configuration that applies to it:
  - List repository organization secrets.
  - List repository organization variables.

Overall, the `actions` API enables:
- Central policy and permissions management (allowed actions, workflow access, approvals).
- Fine-grained configuration and scoping for secrets/variables.
- Complete lifecycle control over workflow runs, jobs, logs, and artifacts.
- Full management of both GitHub-hosted and self-hosted runners, including grouping, access control, provisioning, and labeling, at both organization and repository levels.
- Control of cache limits, storage, and usage across enterprise, organization, and repository scopes.
